rows.description: Soluta Varia 1766 Brod Rec
rows.notes: Page title / heading at top left. There is a number group at the far right on the same line (looks like 674 8 2) but this line functions as the page heading; per instructions header rows should be treated as structural labels. Title text is partly faded and 'Brod Rec' is inferred from handwriting.

rows.description: Decrement Domus for last 1/2 year
rows.notes: Description legible. Pounds/shillings/pence columns located by alignment with lower totals. Shillings 18 and pence 10 are clearly in their columns; handwriting of 'Decrement Domus' is a little stylized but readable.
rows.english_description: Household charge (decrement of the house) for the last half-year
rows.english_description_with_amount: Household charge for the last half-year, amounting to £20 18s 10d.

rows.description: Scarbrough for a Bell Rope
rows.notes: Amount 0-4-6 (4s 6d) clearly placed. Description is less certain — first word reads as 'Scarbrook' / 'Scarbrough' / 'Scarsbrook'; chosen reading based on letter shapes but uncertain.
rows.english_description: Payment to Scarbrough for a bell rope
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Scarbrough for a bell rope, amounting to £0 4s 6d.

rows.description: Common Room Acco for Wine &c
rows.notes: Amount 2-1-0 clear. Description 'Common Room Acco' (account) and 'for Wine &c' readable; handwriting clear enough to be confident.
rows.english_description: Common room account expenditure for wine and sundries
rows.english_description_with_amount: Common room account expenditure for wine and sundries, amounting to £2 1s 0d.

rows.description: Watchman for 1 Day &c
rows.notes: Very clear: amount 0-2-6 (2s 6d). Description 'Watchman for 1 Day &c' is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Wages paid to the watchman for one day's service and sundries
rows.english_description_with_amount: Wages paid to the watchman for one day's service and sundries, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.description: Seven Months Poor Taxes & Goal Tax
rows.notes: Amount 3-5-2 clear. 'Seven months Poor Taxes & Goal (Gaol) Tax' interpretation fits the handwriting; 'Goal' spelled as in the manuscript.
rows.english_description: Poor rates and gaol (jail) tax for seven months
rows.english_description_with_amount: Poor rates and gaol tax for seven months, amounting to £3 5s 2d.

rows.description: Bell for Repairs in Merton Church
rows.notes: Amount 0-11-6 readable. Description 'Bell for Repairs in Merton Church' legible; 'Merton' clear.
rows.english_description: Payment toward repairs to a bell in Merton church
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment toward repairs to a bell in Merton church, amounting to £0 11s 6d.

rows.description: To Mr. Fletcher a years Subscrn for Dr Holmes
rows.notes: Amount 5-5-0 aligned clearly. Description partially indistinct in middle words; read as 'To Mr. Fletcher a year's subscrn for Dr Holmes' — words inferred from context and common ledger phrasing; moderate uncertainty about exact spelling.
rows.english_description: One year's subscription paid via Fletcher on behalf of Dr Holmes
rows.english_description_with_amount: One year's subscription paid via Fletcher on behalf of Dr Holmes, amounting to £5 5s 0d.

rows.description: Collation of Septal Version of the Testamt
rows.notes: Description faint but appears to refer to collation of a version of the Testament. No amount visible on the right for this single line (it appears grouped with a following payment by a brace). Because no explicit amount on this row, recorded as an entry with empty amount fields. Low confidence due to faded script and grouping mark.
rows.english_description: Payment for the collation of a version of the Testament (textual collation work)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for the collation of a version of the Testament (textual collation work), amounting to £0 0s 0d.

rows.description: Paid to Mr. Glubb toward Augmt of Merton Vice by Consent
rows.notes: Large amount clearly reads 100-0-0 in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. The description is partly uncertain but 'Paid to Mr Glubb toward Augmt of Merton vice by Consent' matches letter shapes and the brace that connects earlier lines.
rows.english_description: Payment to Glubb toward an augmentation for the Merton vicarage by consent
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Glubb toward an augmentation for the Merton vicarage by consent, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: Padbury for Painting Bursary Parchments
rows.notes: Amount 0-6-6 clear. 'Padbury' (or 'Padbery') and 'Painting Bursary Parchments' reading fits handwriting; moderate confidence on spelling of the name.
rows.english_description: Payment to Padbury for painting bursary parchments
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Padbury for painting bursary parchments, amounting to £0 6s 6d.

rows.description: Mr Chas Curtis for Library Bill
rows.notes: Amount 8-15-2 legible. Description 'Mr Chas Curtis for Library Bill' clear; 'Chas' is the common abbreviation for Charles.
rows.english_description: Payment to Charles Curtis for a library bill (books/supplies)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Charles Curtis for a library bill, amounting to £8 15s 2d.

rows.description: Mr Hart Expences of Inst: to Merton Vice
rows.notes: Amount 9-0-0 clear. 'Mr Hart Expenses of Inst to Merton vice' approximated from script; moderate uncertainty on exact words but contextually consistent.
rows.english_description: Expenses of Mr Hart for instituting (installation) to the Merton vicarage
rows.english_description_with_amount: Expenses of Mr Hart for instituting to the Merton vicarage, amounting to £9 0s 0d.

rows.description: Mr Hart for a 2 - 100 Augmt of Merton vice
rows.notes: Another large 100-0-0 payment clearly visible. Description reads as payment to Mr Hart towards a '2 - 100 Augmentation' for Merton; interpretation of '2 - 100' kept literal as in manuscript.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Hart for a £200 augmentation of the Merton vicarage (recorded as 2–100)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Hart for a £100 augmentation of the Merton vicarage, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Rhodes laid out on Merton Re: House
rows.notes: Amount 0-3-0 clear. Surname may be 'Rosdeus' or 'Rhodes' — read as 'Rhodes' tentatively. 'laid out on Merton Re: House' is inferred from context and abbreviated words.
rows.english_description: Expenses laid out by Mr Rhodes on the Merton rectory/house
rows.english_description_with_amount: Expenses laid out by Mr Rhodes on the Merton rectory/house, amounting to £0 3s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Regand Expences of servd Wittenham
rows.notes: Amount 1-12-9 visible. Person's name and some words (Regand / Regand?) are uncertain; 'Wittenham' is legible. Moderate uncertainty on exact name spelling.
rows.english_description: Expenses of Mr Regand connected with serving the parish of Wittenham
rows.english_description_with_amount: Expenses of Mr Regand connected with serving Wittenham, amounting to £1 12s 9d.

rows.description: To Mr Davis 1/2 years Attendance in Library
rows.notes: Amount 6-6-0 clear. Description reads 'To Mr. Davis 1/2 years Attendance in Library' with reasonable legibility.
rows.english_description: Half-year payment to Mr Davis for attendance in the library
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year payment to Mr Davis for attendance in the library, amounting to £6 6s 0d.

rows.description: The same brickbills & Hiles, &c.
rows.notes: This appears to be a continuation/sub-item ('The same brickbills & Hiles etc') with no amount on this line; the amount likely sits on a sibling row (braced). Faded and abbreviated; low confidence in exact wording.
rows.english_description: Additional brick and tile bills and related building charges as above
rows.english_description_with_amount: Additional brick and tile bills and related building charges as above, amounting to £0 0s 0d.

rows.description: Allowed half for Building wall out of Wittenham
rows.notes: Amount 6-18-5 in clear alignment. Description 'Allowed half for Building wall at Wittenham' interpreted from handwriting; 'Wittenham' is legible, 'allowed half' phrase inferred but consistent.
rows.english_description: College allowed half the cost for building a wall charged to Wittenham
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowed half the cost for building a wall charged to Wittenham, amounting to £6 18s 5d.

rows.description: Vice by consent of sale a p[lot]
rows.notes: This short line is fragmentary and faint; it appears to be a note 'vice by consent of sale a p[lot]' or similar with no separate amount. Very uncertain; left amount fields empty.
rows.english_description: Record of the vice's consent to the sale of a plot
rows.english_description_with_amount: Record of the vice's consent to the sale of a plot, amounting to £0 0s 0d.

rows.description: Prayer Books for Chapel in 4to
rows.notes: Amount 3-7-6 clear; description 'Prayer Books for Chapel in 4to' legible and consistent with book purchases.
rows.english_description: Purchase of prayer books in quarto for the college chapel
rows.english_description_with_amount: Purchase of prayer books in quarto for the college chapel, amounting to £3 7s 6d.

rows.description: Kidlington Rent Bp: Synodal & Prosecution
rows.notes: Amount 0-9-0 clear. Description beginning 'Kidlington' is legible; remainder of the phrase (Bp Synodal & Provocation/Prosecution) is partially abraded — reading based on letter shapes and common ledger language.
rows.english_description: Kidlington rent: payment of bishop's synodal fees and prosecution costs
rows.english_description_with_amount: Kidlington rent payment for bishop's synodal fees and prosecution costs, amounting to £0 9s 0d.

rows.description: To Hewlett in Part of Acct

rows.english_description: Payment in part to Hewlett on account (part settlement of his bill)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment in part to Hewlett on account, amounting to £10 10s 0d.

rows.description: Mr Lock for Cole Plate as by Bill

rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Lock for college plate (per bill)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Lock for college plate as per bill, amounting to £13 17s 7d.

rows.description: Domus Wine Different from Seal Money
rows.notes: Amount 1-1-0 present but the description is somewhat obscure: reads 'Domus Wine different from seal money' — this is an interpretation of abbreviated/faint script; moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: House wine expense accounted separately from seal money
rows.english_description_with_amount: House wine expense accounted separately from seal money, amounting to £1 1s 0d.

rows.description: Hewlett in Part of Acct
rows.notes: Amount 5-5-0 clear. This appears to be a second line paying 'Hewlett' (same name as earlier). Text is similar to row 23; moderate confidence on exact phrasing.
rows.english_description: Further payment in part to Hewlett on account
rows.english_description_with_amount: Further payment in part to Hewlett on account, amounting to £5 5s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Jno Tulb

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to John Tulb, amounting to £4 1s 0d.

rows.description: To Parker Smith

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Parker Smith, amounting to £4 16s 10d.

rows.description: To Benson Gardiner &c
rows.notes: Amount 16-9-3 clear. The first name looks like 'Benson' or 'Benson/Genson'; 'Gardiner' (Gardener/Gardiner) is legible. Moderate confidence on the given names.
rows.english_description: Payment to Benson and Gardiner (likely servants/grounds staff) and others

rows.description: To Hewlett Plasterer Bond of Acct

rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Hewlett, the plasterer, in settlement of his account
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Hewlett, the plasterer, in settlement of his account, amounting to £37 13s 2d.

rows.description: To Mr. Pearce as by Bill

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Pearce as stated in his bill, amounting to £54 0s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Hart Prayer(?) Expenses
rows.notes: Amount 19-13-0 visible. The phrase after 'Mr Hart' is faded and could be 'preach expenses' or 'paroch expenses'; chosen 'Prayer(?) Expenses' to reflect uncertainty. Moderate-low confidence on the descriptive words.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Hart for prayer or chapel-related expenses
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Hart for prayer or chapel-related expenses, amounting to £19 13s 0d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Hubbard for fees, amounting to £1 7s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 0-6-0 legible. Name 'Walker Cooper' clear in script; common pairing (Walker is likely surname or given name + Cooper) readable.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Small payment to Walker Cooper, likely for coopering or related services, amounting to £0 6s 0d.

rows.description: Bell Rope
rows.notes: Amount 0-4-6 visible (second instance of a bell rope payment). Single-word description 'Bell Rope' faint but readable; could be repetition of earlier bell-rope expense.
rows.english_description: Purchase or repair of a bell rope for the college
rows.english_description_with_amount: Purchase or repair of a bell rope for the college, amounting to £0 4s 6d.

rows.description: To Mr. Glubb as by Bill
rows.notes: Amount 2-16-6 clear. 'Mr Glubb as by Bill' transcription follows letter shapes; moderate confidence on surname spelling but amount alignment is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Glubb as specified in his bill
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Glubb as specified in his bill, amounting to £2 16s 6d.

rows.notes: Bottom-right running total reads '1157 8 11 1/2' (pounds 1157, 8s, 11½d). The halfpenny symbol is visible as a small loop after the pence, interpreted as 0.5. Alignment with column rules checked: shillings 8 (0-19), pence 11 (0-11) with .5 fraction valid.
